name: Mechanical Ventilation Calculator
seo_description: Calculate required airflow and fan size for mechanical ventilation by room volume and air changes per hour. Free tool with instant results.
example_label: Calculate mechanical ventilation for a 90m2 home with 2.5m ceiling height.

range_hints.area_m2: Floor area to ventilate. Residential: 50-150 m², Office: 100-500 m², Commercial: 200-1000 m²
range_hints.altura_m: Ceiling height. Residential: 2.5-2.7 m, Commercial/Industrial: 3-5 m
range_hints.renovaciones_hora: Homes: 0.5-0.8 ACH, Offices: 1-2 ACH, Kitchens: 2-3 ACH, Garages: 4-6 ACH

result_context: Vivienda of {area_m2}m² with {height_m}m of height requires ventilador of {caudal_m3h} m³/h ({caudal_ls} L/s) to {renovaciones_hora} renovaciones/hour.
formula_display: Volume = area × height; Caudal = volume × renovaciones_hora
steps: Required air changes: 0.5 per hour = 112.5 m3/h
Apply standard: CTE DB-HS3 requires 5 L/s per person
mistakes: Undersizing ventilation for rooms without windows
Not accounting for kitchen and bathroom extract requirements
Forgetting air intake for balanced ventilation systems
description: Calculate required airflow for ventilating a space.
rc: With a calculated demand of {carga_total} A on a {tamano_panel} A panel, you have {margen}% spare capacity.
fd: Demand = sum of breakers × diversity factor; Spare = (panel − demand) ÷ panel × 100
el: Panel load check for a house with 10 circuits on a 100 A panel
desc: Calculate required airflow for ventilating a space.
